ops.text:
RESULTS (E18) — model fit + strata + row lists. Spend $0.00 (pure compute; no LLM calls).

MODELED SET: 1623 repeat-vote items / 4456 opus judge votes (413 items x2 [v5,v6]; 1210 x3 [v4,v5,v6]). Model held constant (opus); repeated votes are across prompt_versions 4/5/6.

INSTRUMENT AUDIT (headline — the vote cache is NOT randomly noisy):
- Fleiss kappa 0.79 (3-vote) / 0.73 (2-vote) = substantial agreement.
- Beta-Binomial fit alpha=0.135 beta=0.145, ICC=0.78, overdispersion LRT=2130 (p~0): item verdicts are highly concentrated (U-shaped), disagreement is localized not diffuse.
- Irreducible judge-noise floor = 3.6% (3-vote non-monotone flips [26] + symmetric 2-vote down-flips). Observed steer-flip 15.0%; category-churn 29.5%.
- DRIFT vs NOISE decomposition: v4->v5 transitions symmetric (30 up / 28 down, McNemar p=0.90 = noise). v5->v6 asymmetric 196 up / 16 down, McNemar p=1.5e-40 = SYSTEMATIC EXPANSION. v6 steer-rate 0.547 vs 0.44 (v4/v5). So ~80% of the 15% flip is a deliberate v5->v6 prompt broadening, only ~3.6% is irreducible noise.
- CATEGORY -> is_steering is DETERMINISTIC in the corpus (direction/wrong_approach/incorrect_change/unwanted_action/style_violation/premature=steering; operational_directive/status_update/new_task/question/other=non-steering). A steer-flip == a category move across the steering boundary.
- Dominant instability: v6 reclassifies operational_directive (informational, non-steering) -> direction (steering): 90 items; +operational_directive->wrong_approach 33; +new_task->direction 15. This operational_directive/direction boundary is THE E19 target.

RANKED PROBLEM STRATA (n>=15, flip-rate Wilson-LB > global 0.15), by EB-shrunk flip:
 1. v6_cat=direction | L1_short   n=35  flip .97 [.85,.99] churn 1.00 drift .94 nonmono .06 kappa -0.34
 2. v6_cat=direction (all)        n=129 flip .91 [.84,.95] churn 1.00 drift .98 nonmono .02 kappa -0.21
 3. direction | L4_xlong          n=27  flip .93  drift 1.00
 4. direction | L2_med            n=38  flip .87  drift 1.00
 5. direction | L3_long           n=29  flip .86  drift 1.00
 6. repo=yclaw                    n=46  flip .37 [.25,.51] drift 1.00 kappa 0.42
 7. incorrect_change | L1_short   n=28  flip .29  drift .75
 8. repo=dailies                  n=40  flip .28 [.16,.43] drift .82 kappa 0.63
All 8 problem strata are DRIFT-dominated (v6 up-flips), NOT noise — negative kappa on direction = systematic version split, not stochastic disagreement. Context-length shows no monotone effect; direction is unstable at every band.

E13 SHADOW CROSS-REF: all 51 dual-shadow proposals are dual-scored (gate_score+sentinel_prob). yclaw = 5 proposals AND a flagged unreliable judge stratum (flip 0.37) -> E13 yclaw proposals warrant re-judging. Other proposal repos (cookie-sync .065, stream-trade .064, cc-runtime .111) judge-reliable; 34 proposals have no project (uncrossable).

ROW-ID DELIVERABLES (out/):
- e20_label_noise_items.csv: 44 items = irreducible label noise (non-monotone + against-drift down-flips) -> E20 clean-label cross-ref. These are the ONLY genuinely mislabeled rows.
- e19_frontier_items.csv: 478 items (steer-flip OR category-churn) = prompt-hardening frontier -> E19.
- problem_rows/*.json: per-stratum row lists split into e19_prompt_frontier_rows + e20_label_noise_rows (8 strata files).
- problem_rows.json: combined stratum -> {e19_frontier_keys, e20_noise_keys}.
- strata_table.csv/json: all 68 reported strata (v6_category, length_band, repo, source_kind, cat×len) ranked.
- model_summary.json + addendum.json: global fit, drift/noise decomposition, category map, transitions, shadow crossref.

SUCCESS CRITERION MET: (1) per-item reliability model fit (Beta-Binomial + Fleiss kappa) with reported noise floor (3.6%); (2) ranked strata table, 8 strata exceed floor with n>=15 and Wilson-LB>global; (3) row-id lists written for E19/E20.

CAVEAT for downstream: "disagreement" here is 80% intentional v5->v6 prompt evolution, not judge unreliability. E20 must NOT treat all 478 flip items as label noise — only the 44 in e20_label_noise_items.csv. E19 should adjudicate the operational_directive<->direction boundary (is v6's expansion right or over-triggering?), which no amount of repeated voting resolves (it's a definition, not a measurement).

Scripts: E18-vote-reliability/scripts/{extract,model,finalize}.py (import numpy/scipy from workspace venv cc-steer-exp0; no repo mutations).
